llm_result.reasoning: Plastic housing for electronic devices falls under Chapter 35, heading 2, subheading 90, as it is a container or casing for an electrical device.

results.gold_reasoning: The plastic housing is specifically designed to contain electronic circuit boards. By GRI 1 and Note 1(g) to Chapter 39, articles of plastic which are parts of goods of Chapter 85 are classified in Chapter 85. Essential character is electronic.

results.description: Metal frame with rubber mounting for machinery

llm_result.reasoning: Rubber mounts are classified under Chapter 45 (Raw hides and skins) but since they are mounted on a metal frame, it falls under Chapter 47 (Articles of steel or iron). The specific product is a machinery support structure with rubber mounts, which aligns with the classification of 'other articles of steel or iron'.

results.gold_reasoning: Classified as a part of machinery under heading 8431 (parts suitable for use solely or principally with the machinery of headings 8425 to 8430). The rubber mounts indicate purpose-built mounting, not a general steel article.

results.description: Ceramic coffee mug with electronic heating element

llm_result.reasoning: The ceramic coffee mug is classified under Chapter 35 (Articles of ceramics, porcelain, and other potsherds) and subheading 3536.90.10, as it is a ceramic article with no specific functional features that would require classification in another chapter.

results.gold_reasoning: Electric heating appliance. Ceramic is incidental; essential character is the electro-thermic function. Note 1(u) to Chapter 85 confirms Chapter 69 ceramic articles fitted with electrical features fall in Chapter 85.

llm_result.reasoning: The product is a USB flash drive with a plastic shell, which falls under Chapter 35 of the Harmonized System, subheading 35.02.10.00, covering other articles for the same use as data carriers.

results.gold_reasoning: Solid-state non-volatile storage device. Heading 8523 covers semiconductor media. Despite the plastic enclosure, the essential character is the storage medium. Plastic shell is incidental packaging.

results.description: Software license key, printed on paper card

regex_result.reasoning: Keyword 'printed/paper card' matches 4901 (printed matter). Some LLMs incorrectly associate 'software' with recording media even when no media is shipped.

llm_result.reasoning: Software license key printed on a paper card is classified as a printed matter, specifically a document, under Chapter 49 of the Harmonized System.

results.llm_pitfall: LLMs tend to default to 8523 or 8471 because the description mentions 'software'. Correct answer requires knowing that the carrier matters when no medium is present.
results.gold_reasoning: Printed matter. Per Note 1(m) to Chapter 85 and Section XV Note 6, software delivered as printed license code without physical media is classified by the carrier (paper) under heading 4901. Many LLMs incorrectly jump to 8523 (recording media) or 8471 (automatic data processing) by associating with software.

regex_result.reasoning: Direct match: portable automatic data processing machine.

llm_result.reasoning: Refurbished laptop computer is classified under Chapter 35, Section X of the Harmonized System, subheading 3505.10.90, as it falls within the scope of used or refurbished computers.

results.llm_pitfall: LLMs may flag 'refurbished' as requiring a different code or may incorrectly apply used-goods subheadings when classification is identical to new.
results.gold_reasoning: Portable automatic data processing machine. Refurbished units keep the same HS classification as new (Note 1(a) to Chapters 84-85 does not exclude used/refurbished goods). Some LLMs incorrectly apply 'used' surcharges or different headings for refurbished electronics.
